# Oraclous

> Oraclous is an open-source agentic AI operations platform — a "second mind" where human members and AI Agents work as one governed fabric under your organisation's own ReBAC access rules, with BYOM model choice and OHM portability.

Oraclous reframes agentic AI from "orchestrate a bag of bots" to "form a second mind": the organisation's combined human and Agent capacity, governed as one fabric. An Operator describes a goal in plain language; the platform compiles it into a governed Harness, runs it across human and AI Agent Actors, and proves every step. It is data-sovereign by design (cross-organisation data flow is structurally impossible), model-agnostic via BYOM (Anthropic-native, OpenAI-compatible, Gemini-compatible), portable through the open OHM manifest, and open source. Terminology is exact and brand-owned: Harness, OHM, ReBAC (never RBAC), BYOM, Operator, Agent (never "bot"), Capability, Consciousness, Portability. The platform is pre/early — proof is the architecture, the open code, and honest docs, not customer logos or benchmarks; ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II are roadmap (target) certifications, not yet held.

## Core

- [Home](https://oraclous.com/): What Oraclous is, the "second mind" thesis, and the "three bad choices" (bespoke code, closed SaaS, framework wiring) Oraclous replaces as the fourth choice.
- [Platform overview](https://oraclous.com/platform): The one governed fabric — Harnesses carry the work, ReBAC governs it, BYOM runs it, OHM keeps it portable. Index of all eleven capabilities.
- [How it works](https://oraclous.com/how-it-works): The goal-to-governed-work lifecycle in five steps — Describe, Compile, Govern, Run, Learn — with every step audited.
- [Glossary](https://oraclous.com/glossary): Canonical definitions of every Oraclous term (Harness, OHM, Operator, Capability, Consciousness, second mind) and category terms (ReBAC, MCP, BYOM, agentic AI, data sovereignty). The entity source of truth.

## Platform capabilities

- [Harness & OHM](https://oraclous.com/platform/harness-model): The unit of work — a goal-driven assembly of human and Agent Actors under one policy envelope; OHM is its portable serialised manifest.
- [Actors](https://oraclous.com/platform/actors): Humans and AI Agents as symmetric Actors on one task board, each with identity, scope, and capability allocation.
- [Compile](https://oraclous.com/platform/compile): How an Operator's plain-language goal becomes a reviewable, governed Harness — survey, clarify, plan, emit manifest, review, commit.
- [ReBAC governance](https://oraclous.com/platform/rebac-governance): Access by relationship, not role, enforced platform-wide at the Harness level under five named, versioned policy sets; code overrides prose.
- [BYOM](https://oraclous.com/platform/byom): Bring your own model provider in three protocol shapes (Anthropic-native, OpenAI-compatible, Gemini-compatible); switch by config without rewriting the Harness.
- [Knowledge graph & retrieval](https://oraclous.com/platform/knowledge-graph): Queryable, provenance-tracked organisational memory; ReBAC-bounded semantic, full-text, hybrid, and graph-traversal queries.
- [Human-in-the-loop](https://oraclous.com/platform/human-in-the-loop): Human review as a first-class runtime primitive — the runtime treats waiting on a human like waiting on a tool return.
- [Execution & scheduling](https://oraclous.com/platform/execution-scheduling): Durable, checkpointed runs that survive restarts; schedules fire from Harness triggers; timeouts and retry caps prevent runaway loops.
- [MCP & widgets](https://oraclous.com/platform/mcp-widgets): Oraclous as both MCP server (exposes ReBAC-scoped capabilities to clients like Claude Desktop) and MCP client (imports external tools as native OHM), plus embeddable widgets.
- [Portability](https://oraclous.com/platform/portability): Leave without a rewrite via OHM and the open reference runtime — with the limits stated plainly (credentials, ReBAC graph, and Consciousness records do not travel).
- [Metering](https://oraclous.com/platform/metering): Substrate-level neutral measurement of tokens, tool calls, storage, time, and traversals — counts and bytes, never prices; you set the rates.

## Solutions

- [For operations](https://oraclous.com/solutions/operations): Ship governed workflows without an engineering sprint per use case — automate the work, not the engineering backlog.
- [For developers](https://oraclous.com/solutions/developers): Build on a substrate that already provides ReBAC, credentials, provenance, and metering — compose Capabilities, not plumbing.
- [For regulated industries](https://oraclous.com/solutions/regulated): Prove control structurally — org isolation, your keys, ReBAC, provenance, self-host or air-gapped, identical guarantees.
- [For multi-model teams](https://oraclous.com/solutions/multi-model): Change the model, keep the Harness — BYOM across three protocol shapes, providers side by side, keys under your own KMS.

## Security & open source

- [Security & data sovereignty](https://oraclous.com/security): The trust model a security reviewer reads before procurement — structural isolation, customer-held keys, ReBAC, provenance, policy-set budget caps. ISO/SOC stated as roadmap, not certified.
- [Open source](https://oraclous.com/open-source): What is open (the whole platform, not a teaser), what is deliberately left out, how decisions are recorded as ADRs, and the self-host story.
- [Honest roadmap](https://oraclous.com/honest-roadmap): What Oraclous deliberately does not do — deferred features, non-goals, and the commitments it will never break (no unbounded agent autonomy, no hidden behaviour, no cross-customer data-mining).

## Developers

## Company

- [About](https://oraclous.com/about): What Oraclous is as a company, why it exists, the "second mind" thesis, how it builds in the open, and what it deliberately does not claim.

## Key facts

- Category: open-source agentic AI / multi-agent orchestration platform ("agentic operations platform").
- Core thesis: form an organisation's "second mind" — humans and AI Agents as symmetric Actors on one governed fabric.
- Governance: ReBAC (relationship-based access control), enforced platform-wide at the Harness level under five named, versioned policy sets; coded enforcement always overrides prose.
- Model choice: BYOM in three protocol shapes — Anthropic-native, OpenAI-compatible, Gemini-compatible.
- Interoperability: first-class MCP (Model Context Protocol) as both server and client.
- Data sovereignty: every record carries an `organization_id`; cross-organisation data flow is structurally impossible; in cloud mode KMS separation means Oraclous staff cannot decrypt customer state (ADR-008).
- Portability: every export routes through OHM (Oraclous Harness Manifest); the reference runtime is open; limits are documented (credentials, ReBAC graph, Consciousness records do not travel).
- Deployment: self-host (free, open source) or cloud-hosted (paid, usage-based) — identical data-sovereignty guarantees.
- Compliance: ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II are target (roadmap) certifications for cloud-hosted mode — NOT certified today.
- Maturity: pre/early — no published customers, logos, or benchmarks. Proof is the architecture, open code, and honest docs.

## Contact

- Website: https://oraclous.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/OraclousAI
- Full reference for AI ingestion: https://oraclous.com/llms-full.txt
